title: Preparation of fresh paraformaldehyde for mouse perfusion and tissue fixation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"na","text":"Hnasko lab - preparation of fresh PFA for ms perfusion and tissue fixation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1n7b","text":"Caution: Paraformaldehyde is toxic. Please read the MSDS before\nworking with this chemical. Gloves, mask and safety glasses should be worn and\nsolutions made inside a fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2761c","text":"paraformaldehyde (PFA, EMS, cat# 19210)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vcfi","text":"10X PBS (Fisher bioreagents, cat# BP399-1)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"brnpf","text":"vacuum filtration flask (ThermoScientific, cat# 567-0020).\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1.For 1 L of 4% paraformaldehyde, warm up 700 mL of ddH2O in a glass beaker using microwave until 40-60 °C .
2.Place on heated stir plate in a chemical fume hood, add a magnetic stir bar and start stirring and insert thermometer into solution.
3.Weigh 40 g of granular paraformaldehyde (PFA, EMS, cat# 19210).

4.Add PFA to the heated ddH2O solution and place on a stir plate in a chemical fume hood. Insert thermometer. Stir and heat to approximately 60 °C (paraformaldehyde flash point = 71°C). Avoid overheating.
5.The powder will not immediately dissolve into solution. Slowly raise the pH by adding about 1 mL NaOH 10N until the solution clears.
6.Once the paraformaldehyde is dissolved, add 100 mL 10X PBS (Fisher bioreagents, cat# BP399-1).
7.Cool solution to Room temperature before adjusting pH, can place on ice to speed cooling.
8.Adjust pH to ~ pH 7.4 with small amounts of 12N HCl.
9.Adjust the volume of the solution to 1 L with ddH2O.
10.Filter the solution using a vacuum filtration flask (ThermoScientific, cat# 567-0020). Label and store in sealed container at 4 °C .
creator: amanda schneeweis
